Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.utils

Class Iso8601Utils

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.marshall.utils.Iso8601Utils
public final class Iso8601Utils extends Object
Centralized ISO 8601 formatting and parsing utility for date/time and Duration types.

Provides the built-in serialization format for Temporal, Calendar, Date, XMLGregorianCalendar, and Duration objects.

See Also:
  • Marshalling

  • Method Details

    • format

      public static String format(Object value, ClassMeta<?> type, TimeZone timeZone)
      Formats a date/time or Duration value to its ISO 8601 string representation.

      Convenience dispatcher that uses default per-type wire formats; callers that need format-aware output should use the per-type helpers directly: formatDate(java.util.Date, org.apache.juneau.marshall.ClassMeta<?>, org.apache.juneau.marshall.DateFormat, java.util.TimeZone), formatCalendar(java.lang.Object, org.apache.juneau.marshall.ClassMeta<?>, org.apache.juneau.marshall.CalendarFormat, java.util.TimeZone), formatTemporal(java.time.temporal.TemporalAccessor, org.apache.juneau.marshall.ClassMeta<?>, org.apache.juneau.marshall.TemporalFormat, java.util.TimeZone), formatDuration(java.time.Duration, org.apache.juneau.marshall.DurationFormat), formatPeriod(java.time.Period, org.apache.juneau.marshall.PeriodFormat).

      Parameters:
      value - The value to format.
      type - The class metadata for the value (used for selecting the appropriate formatter for Temporal types).
      timeZone - The session time zone (used when the value lacks zone info).
      Returns:
      The ISO 8601 string representation.

    • formatDuration

      public static String formatDuration(Duration value, DurationFormat format)
      Formats a Duration value using the supplied wire format.
      Parameters:
      value - The value to format. null returns null.
      format - The duration wire format. May be null (defaults to DurationFormat.ISO_8601_WITH_DAYS).
      Returns:
      The formatted value, or null if value is null.

    • formatPeriod

      public static String formatPeriod(Period value, PeriodFormat format)
      Formats a Period value using the supplied wire format.
      Parameters:
      value - The value to format. null returns null.
      format - The period wire format. May be null (defaults to PeriodFormat.ISO_8601).
      Returns:
      The formatted value, or null if value is null.

    • formatDate

      public static String formatDate(Date value, ClassMeta<?> sourceType, DateFormat format, TimeZone timeZone)
      Formats a Date value using the supplied wire format.

      java.sql.Date, java.sql.Time, and java.sql.Timestamp subclasses all dispatch through DateFormat.format(Date, ZoneId) just like Date.

      Parameters:
      value - The value to format. null returns null.
      sourceType - The class metadata for the value (currently unused, kept for symmetry with the parse helper).
      format - The date wire format. May be null (defaults to DateFormat.ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME).
      timeZone - The session time zone (used as the default zone).
      Returns:
      The formatted value, or null if value is null.

    • formatCalendar

      public static String formatCalendar(Object value, ClassMeta<?> sourceType, CalendarFormat format, TimeZone timeZone)
      Formats a Calendar or XMLGregorianCalendar value using the supplied wire format.

      XMLGregorianCalendar values always emit XMLGregorianCalendar.toXMLFormat() regardless of the configured format — mirroring the parse-side rule and the historical wire output. Calendar (including GregorianCalendar) values are formatted using the supplied CalendarFormat.

      Parameters:
      value - The value to format. Must be a Calendar or XMLGregorianCalendar. null returns null.
      sourceType - The class metadata for the value (currently unused, kept for symmetry with the parse helper).
      format - The calendar wire format. May be null (defaults to CalendarFormat.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME).
      timeZone - The session time zone (used as the default zone when the value lacks zone info).
      Returns:
      The formatted value, or null if value is null.

    • formatTemporal

      public static String formatTemporal(TemporalAccessor value, ClassMeta<?> sourceType, TemporalFormat format, TimeZone timeZone)
      Formats a TemporalAccessor value using the supplied wire format.
      Parameters:
      value - The value to format. Must be a Temporal subtype. null returns null.
      sourceType - The class metadata for the value (currently unused, kept for symmetry with the parse helper).
      format - The temporal wire format. May be null (defaults to TemporalFormat.DEFAULT).
      timeZone - The session time zone (used as the default zone when the value lacks zone info).
      Returns:
      The formatted value, or null if value is null.

    • formatAsDate

      public static String formatAsDate(Object value, ClassMeta<?> type, TimeZone timeZone)
      Formats a date/time value as an ISO date (date-only, for OpenAPI 'date' format).
      Parameters:
      value - The value to format.
      type - The class metadata.
      timeZone - The session time zone.
      Returns:
      The ISO date string.

    • formatAsDateTime

      public static String formatAsDateTime(Object value, ClassMeta<?> type, TimeZone timeZone)
      Formats a date/time value as an ISO date-time (for OpenAPI 'date-time' format).
      Parameters:
      value - The value to format.
      type - The class metadata.
      timeZone - The session time zone.
      Returns:
      The ISO date-time string.

    • parse

      public static <T> T parse(String iso8601, ClassMeta<T> targetType, TimeZone timeZone)
      Parses an ISO 8601 string into the specified date/time target type.

      Dispatches to the appropriate per-type parser based on the target's ClassMeta category: parseDate, parseCalendar, parseTemporal, parseDuration, or parsePeriod — each using its default wire format.

      Type Parameters:
      T - The target type.
      Parameters:
      iso8601 - The ISO 8601 string to parse. null returns null.
      targetType - The target class metadata.
      timeZone - The session time zone (used for types that need a default zone).
      Returns:
      The parsed object, or null if iso8601 is null or the target type is not a recognized date/time/duration/period type.

    • parseDuration

      public static Duration parseDuration(String iso8601, DurationFormat formatHint)
      Parses an ISO 8601 / numeric wire value into a Duration.

      Handles ISO 8601 forms ("PT1H", "-PT30M", "PT-6H", "PT1.5S", …) as well as the bare-number wire forms produced by DurationFormat.MILLIS, DurationFormat.SECONDS, DurationFormat.NANOS, and DurationFormat.HOCON (e.g. "500ms", "1.5s").

      The formatHint disambiguates bare integer strings that could be milliseconds, nanos, or seconds — for all other numeric or ISO forms the input is self-describing and the hint is ignored.

      Parameters:
      iso8601 - The wire value to parse. null or empty returns null.
      formatHint - The duration wire format used to disambiguate bare integer inputs. May be null (defaults to DurationFormat.MILLIS).
      Returns:
      The parsed Duration, or null if iso8601 is null or empty.

    • parsePeriod

      public static Period parsePeriod(String value, PeriodFormat formatHint)
      Parses an ISO 8601 / numeric wire value into a Period.

      Handles standard ISO 8601 period strings ("P1Y2M3D", …) as well as the bare-integer wire form produced by PeriodFormat.DAYS.

      Parameters:
      value - The wire value to parse. null or empty returns null.
      formatHint - The period wire format used to interpret bare integers. May be null (defaults to PeriodFormat.DAYS).
      Returns:
      The parsed Period, or null if value is null or empty.

    • parseCalendar

      public static <T> T parseCalendar(String iso8601, ClassMeta<T> targetType, CalendarFormat formatHint, TimeZone timeZone)
      Parses an ISO 8601 wire value into a Calendar or XMLGregorianCalendar.

      Dispatches by target type:

      • XMLGregorianCalendar targets always use DatatypeFactory.newXMLGregorianCalendar(String) and ignore formatHint.
      • Calendar / GregorianCalendar targets honor formatHint when set to anything other than CalendarFormat.NOT_SET or CalendarFormat.ISO_OFFSET_DATE_TIME; the default path uses content-based formatter autodetection.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The target type.
      Parameters:
      iso8601 - The wire value to parse. null returns null.
      targetType - The target class metadata. Must represent a Calendar or XMLGregorianCalendar.
      formatHint - The calendar wire format. May be null (treated as CalendarFormat.NOT_SET).
      timeZone - The session time zone (used as the default zone when the wire value lacks zone info).
      Returns:
      The parsed value, or null if iso8601 is null or the target is not a recognized calendar type.

    • parseDate

      public static <T> T parseDate(String iso8601, ClassMeta<T> targetType, DateFormat formatHint, TimeZone timeZone)
      Parses an ISO 8601 wire value into a Date.

      Honors formatHint when set to anything other than DateFormat.NOT_SET or DateFormat.ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME; the default path uses content-based formatter autodetection.

      Only direct Date targets are produced — java.sql.* subclasses fall through to null, matching the original dispatch.

      Type Parameters:
      T - The target type.
      Parameters:
      iso8601 - The wire value to parse. null returns null.
      targetType - The target class metadata. Must represent Date.
      formatHint - The date wire format. May be null (treated as DateFormat.NOT_SET).
      timeZone - The session time zone (used as the default zone when the wire value lacks zone info).
      Returns:
      The parsed Date, or null if iso8601 is null or the target is not exactly Date.

    • parseTemporal

      public static <T> T parseTemporal(String iso8601, ClassMeta<T> targetType, TemporalFormat formatHint, TimeZone timeZone)
      Parses an ISO 8601 wire value into the specified Temporal subtype.

      Honors formatHint when set to anything other than TemporalFormat.NOT_SET or TemporalFormat.DEFAULT; the default path uses the per-subtype default formatter (e.g. DateTimeFormatter.ISO_INSTANT for Instant, DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE for LocalDate, …).

      Type Parameters:
      T - The target temporal subtype.
      Parameters:
      iso8601 - The wire value to parse. null returns null.
      targetType - The target class metadata. Must represent a Temporal subtype.
      formatHint - The temporal wire format. May be null (treated as TemporalFormat.NOT_SET).
      timeZone - The session time zone (used as the default zone when the wire value lacks zone info).
      Returns:
      The parsed value, or null if iso8601 is null or the target is not a Temporal subtype.

    • fromEpochMillis

      public static <T> T fromEpochMillis(long epochMillis, ClassMeta<T> targetType, TimeZone timeZone)
      Converts epoch milliseconds to the target date/time type.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The target type.
      Parameters:
      epochMillis - The epoch milliseconds value.
      targetType - The target class metadata.
      timeZone - The session time zone.
      Returns:
      The converted date/time object.
